item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a fellow reader, thank you for doing this investigative work, and for posting it in comments. If I could upvote your comment, I would.

To the SCP author: this is an interesting interweaving of literal, semiotic, and metaphorical meaning. I have to appreciate the effort you put into crafting this; it looked an awful lot like complete lolrandom at first, but that would be an unfair characterization. (I sure am glad I checked comments before voting.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey Peppertoast,
first or all, I'd like to say that this skip is a nice little gem, short but descriptive and interesting. I think I'm still missing something, but I believe most of the following is true.

+ show block
– hide block


So, at first I was looking at various obscure numerology things with the numbers of every deer, the number 22, the ages of the Gilmor family and so on.

Then, with the hint of the EXPLOSIONS theme, I looked at the date of the "displacement", 15th May 1955, which at first I thought was simply a reference to the SCP number. By looking up the events on that date, it turns out that on that date took place the last nuclear Test of the so-called "Operation Teapot", right there in Nevada, code name "Zucchini".

So, my current idea is the following: in the Gilmor family there was one (or more) reality benders, and that jolly bunch got (for whatever reason), caught by the Zucchini nuclear explosion: this prompted the reality bender to save its family by creating this weird "Semantic Dimension", which interacts with the baseline in ways both physical and purely semantic, such as the huge amount of zucchini being a transposition of the devastating energy of the zucchini explosion. This semantic connection is extended to all of the house, the things that are inside and its inhabitants. I'm not entirely sure of this, but I suspect that the connections we see are the following:
- Deer 19: Brisbane (btw, Euridyce because she wants to come back from the death and is the only one aware? Nice)
- Deer 21: Rhonda, who's cooking something (Deer 09 & Deer 16, and given the end of the log also Deer 03) by first cutting it and then cooking it (through the oven, which is Deer 01?)
- Deer 07: Sassafras, who runs with Brisbane?
- Deer 14: Suzie
- Deer 04: Edward, with Suzie on his shoulders
- Deer 22: not sure at all. The handle of Deer 01/the oven?

Lastly, so, who's the reality bender? My initial guess was Brisbane, as she's the only one aware and she's in all this biblical frenzy trying to resurrect Jesus, but really I believe it's Edward. He's the one asking what happens to a God/RB when he dies (which is, their fate), he's the one that regrets putting Suzie in this dimension, and he's the one who's 33 years old. Maybe Brisbane got the father's gift and is figuring out how to use it to try to resurrect Jesus/the father from this dimension, don't know. Not even sure if the number 22 and the Deer ones mean anything, would like to know at this point.

So yeah, maybe I'm completely off, but I suspect at least the general idea is there. Let me know Peppermint, and again sincere compliments for the beautiful skip.

– hide block

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know, this mostly makes sense to me.

+ show block
– hide block

There are two "sides" to reality in this situation. On the reader's side is a herd of deer with strange things happening to it. On the other side, possibly in an alternate timeline or dimension, is the Gilmer family.

Somehow, these two aspects of two different realities have been causally linked, such that the actions of one influence the other. This relationship seemingly does not work both ways, however.

In the same way that you'd have problems if you tethered a submarine to a plane, this new relationship has unpleasant side effects for both sides. This is represented by the physical anomalies on the deer side, and the zucchini infestation and/or existential horror on the Gilmer side. Their minds and possibly souls are being worn down as two misfit dimensional gears grind their teeth against one another.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey man, I finally registered "just" for give you upvote and so I can tell that is really amazing little SCP.
It's really "what the damn hell" story, but I think I can see internal logic, although I cannot see the whole thing.

The sentence "Where does God go when he dies?" resonates in my head. It's creepy. It's disturbing. It's amazing. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I'm not sure why, but thinking of a perry bible strip as I read this (the one with the deer laser-vapourising another deer).

Minor Conjectures +
Minor Conjectures -

Is Rhonda's chopping / dicing / grilling zucchini one of the reasons for the, uh, deer translating / rotating / clipping / burning, seeing as they're overflow buffers for, uh, the tons of zucchini (among other things)?

And I can maybe map Edward and Suzie playing to Deers 4/14/15, though how screaming Brisbane maps to the deer is beyond me as yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Translated into Russian. By the way, what's the heck?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While reading the article, I was imagining the deer clipping, and T-posing around the containment area.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can only really understand this in video game metaphors so correct me if im wrong. is this like if you swapped every model in a scene (in this case a family cooking dinner) with the 3d model for a deer?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It gets better every time I read it, as confusing as it is. I have a couple theories as of me writing this review, though I still have no idea what zucchinis have to do with it aside from infesting the property and adding more to obscurity and absurdity of this anomaly.

But I enjoyed it none the less, have an upvote.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry, this just makes no sense to me at all. Most good SCP articles involve the reader making connections or reading between the lines, but this is just meaningless to me. I don't understand it.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Initial thoughts
- Initial thoughts

Blimey, I've gone over this seven times with a set square and theodolite and I still have no idea what's going on. To detract from my cluelessness, please have some errata:

and consequently it is different to predict when and how SCP-5155 may stray from baseline.

difficult to predict?

A figured presumed to be Deer 22 travels across the sky

figure?

I'm also wondering if the deer numbers are correct, because I can't make this -

Dear 04 and Deer 14 lift from the ground and drift away

- jibe with the context. I feel like it should be 04 and 15, but you never know.

If you can confirm that everything is as it should be, then I'll likely have another go at this because it's a puzzle and I must solve it and completionist tendencies you know argh

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image comes from here and is cc-compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really, really hate downvoting stuff during Jam, but several run-throughs of this article really make no sense at all. It's like it is trying to be as random as possible ("unrelated 8.6 tonnes of fresh zucchini") without making any semblance of a point. It just…it goes nowhere.

You mentioned this is a coldpost? It really could have benefited from feedback, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll have the absurdist appetizer, and for the main course, a whole brisket of existential horror marinated in deliriant, please. And can you make it extra Kafkaesque?

Silliness aside — I definitely like this, even if I can't grasp the depth in a single readthrough. But that's what makes it good.

EDIT: Upon reread, what I appreciate most is the imagery/spatial fuckery this makes me imagine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The surreality teeters on the brink of being lolrandom, but it skillfully shows that there is an internal logic here governing what's happening, even if I personally can't wrap my head around it. I think a little more context as to why everything that comes out of the Brisbane family mouths is so bizarre would make this more accessible but the horror of looking into the unknown and only recognizing familiar forms while the actual sights and sounds confound the eye is good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't have a clue what's going on here, but I think I like it. I'll probably come back here and see if I can figure anything out. Either way, +1.

+ minor typo
- minor typo

Deer 21 throw itself against Deer 09's head, causing Deer 09 to […]

Should be throws, unless this is suggesting something I'm not catching up on.

EDIT: Goddamn it, I'm drawing a blank. You roped me in with that zucchini comment and now I can't help but want to know what this is all about.

Also, am I allowed to edit the article if I find any additional typos? I'm not sure on whether that's against the contest rules or not.

item.title: Surprise, bitch. I bet you thought you'd seen the last of me.
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to SCP-5155, in which Peppers fits in one more coldpost for JamCon.

No spoilers from me, but I swear the zucchinis are actually super relevant to what's going on here.

Also, this one uses all three themes from this year. Yes, especially that one.
